UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information, account status, and aggregated statistics within the application. This is the central entity for user-specific data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

school.description: The name of the school the user is associated with.

isPro.description: Indicates whether the user has an active PRO subscription.

creditBalance.description: The current balance of AI generation credits available to the user.

totalLessonPlansCreated.description: A denormalized count of the total number of lesson plans created by this user. (Derived from LessonPlan entity count).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

LessonPlan.title: LessonPlan
LessonPlan.description: Represents a detailed Physical Education lesson plan generated by the AI, owned by a specific user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LessonPlan entity.

title.description: The title or main topic of the lesson plan.

studyYear.description: The academic year level for which the lesson plan is designed (e.g., 'السنة_الأولى').

learningField.description: The specific learning field or domain covered by the lesson plan (e.g., 'الوضعيّات والتنقلات').

knowledgeResource.description: The primary knowledge resource from the curriculum targeted by the lesson plan.

specificResource.description: A more specific element of the knowledge resource if applicable.

objectives.description: A list of SMART objectives generated or selected for this lesson plan.

introductoryStage.description: Content for the preparatory/introductory stage of the lesson plan.

buildingStage.description: Content for the main learning/building stage of the lesson plan.

finalStage.description: Content for the concluding/final stage of the lesson plan.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson plan was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson plan was last updated.

UsageLog.title: UsageLog
UsageLog.description: Records each instance of an AI feature being used by a user, detailing the resources consumed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UsageLog entity.

aiFeatureUsed.description: Describes which AI feature was invoked (e.g., 'Objective Generation', 'Lesson Plan Drafting').

tokensConsumed.description: The raw number of AI tokens used by the generative AI model for this specific request.

creditsBurned.description: The number of user-facing credits deducted from the user's balance for this AI usage event, calculated based on tokens consumed and model multiplier.

modelMultiplier.description: The cost multiplier applied to tokens consumed, reflecting the relative expense of the AI model used (e.g., 1.0 for standard, 1.5 for premium).

lessonPlanId.description: Optional reference to a LessonPlan if this usage log is directly associated with its creation or modification. (Relationship: LessonPlan 0..1:N UsageLog)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this AI usage event was logged.

CreditTransaction.title: CreditTransaction
CreditTransaction.description: Records every change (addition or deduction) to a user's credit balance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CreditTransaction entity.

amount.description: The amount of credits involved in the transaction. Positive for additions (e.g., purchase, initial grant), negative for deductions (e.g., AI usage).

transactionType.description: The category of the credit transaction (e.g., 'AI_Deduction', 'PRO_Purchase', 'Initial_Grant', 'Refund', 'Manual_Adjustment').

description.description: A brief, human-readable description of the transaction.

usageLogId.description: Optional reference to a UsageLog if this transaction is a deduction due to AI usage. (Relationship: UsageLog 0..1:N CreditTransaction)

paymentId.description: Optional reference to an external payment ID (e.g., Chargily transaction ID) if this transaction is an addition from a purchase.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the credit transaction occurred.

definition.description: Represents a user's profile and aggregated account information. The document ID (`{userId}`) MUST match the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes denormalized 'creditBalance' and 'totalLessonPlansCreated' for quick access and efficient security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores individual AI-generated lesson plans. Each lesson plan is owned by a specific user. The 'userId' field in the document provides denormalized ownership context for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this lesson plan.

params.name: lessonPlanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific lesson plan.

definition.description: Records individual AI feature usage events. Each log entry is tied to a specific user and details tokens consumed and credits burned. The 'userId' field in the document provides denormalized ownership context for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed this AI usage.

params.name: usageLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific AI usage log entry.

definition.description: Logs all credit balance changes (additions or deductions) for a user. Each transaction is tied to a specific user. The 'userId' field in the document provides denormalized ownership context for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user associated with this credit transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific credit transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following a user-centric, multi-tenant model to ensure robust security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data is nested under a top-level `users` collection, with each user's data residing in a document named after their Firebase Authentication UID (`/users/{userId}`). This path-based ownership is fundamental for simplifying security rules.

**Authorization Independence:** This design critically achieves Authorization Independence by explicitly including the `userId` field within every document in subcollections (`lessonPlans`, `usageLogs`, `creditTransactions`). For instance, a `LessonPlan` document will have a `userId` field. This denormalization means that security rules for these subcollections do not need to perform `get()` operations on the parent `UserProfile` document to determine ownership. Instead, rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` field of the document being accessed. This significantly enhances rule performance, prevents cascading permission issues, and enables atomic operations (e.g., creating a user profile and their first lesson plan in a single batch operation).

**Support for Query-as-Permission (QAPs):** QAPs are fully supported due to the structural segregation. When a user requests their list of lesson plans, for example, the query will target `/users/{userId}/lessonPlans`. The Firestore security rules can then simply state `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`. Since the path itself already scopes the query to the authenticated user's data, no additional filtering or complex logic is required in the rules, making them simple, efficient, and secure. Similarly, listing `usageLogs` and `creditTransactions` for a specific user follows the same secure pattern.

**Usage Tracking System Implementation:**
*   **API AI Tokens Consumed:** Each AI interaction will create a `UsageLog` document in `/users/{userId}/usageLogs`. This document will store `tokensConsumed` (raw API tokens) and `creditsBurned` (the user-facing credit equivalent based on the conversion logic).
*   **Total Memos Created (Lesson Plans):** The `UserProfile` document includes `totalLessonPlansCreated`, which is a denormalized counter. This counter will be atomically incremented via a Cloud Function or backend process whenever a new `LessonPlan` document is successfully created under `/users/{userId}/lessonPlans/{lessonPlanId}`.
*   **Credit Balance:** The `UserProfile` document contains `creditBalance`. All changes to this balance (deductions from AI usage, additions from PRO subscriptions) will be recorded as `CreditTransaction` documents under `/users/{userId}/creditTransactions`. The `creditBalance` in the `UserProfile` will be updated atomically in conjunction with creating these transaction logs, ensuring consistency and providing a clear audit trail.
*   **1000 Tokens = 1 Credit:** This conversion logic will be applied on the backend when creating `UsageLog` documents, calculating `creditsBurned` and subsequently updating `creditBalance` via `CreditTransaction`.
